content: In C:\Users\mathm\Documents\Projects\Tuff\compiler\index.test.ts, add test case executeTuff("let x = 0U8; let y = &x;") => 0

[Session context summary — replaces earlier history]
The goal was to fix ESLint violations in `index.ts` (specifically `max-lines-per-function` and `prefer-const`) while adding support for comparison operators (`<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `==`, `!=`) and verifying block-scope mutation behavior through new test cases. 

In `C:\Users\mathm\Documents\Projects\Tuff\compiler\index.ts`, the tokenizer logic was extracted into a top-level `tokenize()` function, and helper functions like `normalizeResult` and `combineTypes` were moved outside `executeTuff()`. This reduced the main function's line count below the 250-line ESLint threshold. The expression parser (`parseExprWithType`) was updated to handle comparison operators between arithmetic and logical operations, returning a `"Bool"` type with values of `1n` or `0n`. Stray braces and duplicate definitions from earlier partial edits were cleaned up during this refactor.

In `C:\Users\mathm\Documents\Projects\Tuff\compiler\index.test.ts`, several new tests were added: one verifying that mutating a variable inside a block persists outside it (`let mut x = 0U8; { x = 100U8; } x`), individual tests for all six comparison operators, and a combined test using parentheses to mix comparisons with logical OR. 

Multiple `bun test`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `npx eslint . --ext .ts` commands were run throughout the process. All 47+ tests pass cleanly, TypeScript compiles without errors, and ESLint reports zero violations. No open tasks remain from this session.
Got it, I have the prior context.
